communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all records of email communications, including attachments and calendar invitations, that were sent to or received from the domain “@flocksafety.com,” between November 1, 2025 and the date this request is fulfilled.

This request includes:
- Emails where @flocksafety.com appears in the sender, recipient (To, CC, BCC), or within the body of the message
- Attachments of any file type, including but not limited to PDFs, presentations, spreadsheets, and images
- Calendar invitations, meeting requests, and related responses involving Flock Safety personnel

Please search all agency-controlled email systems and accounts reasonably likely to contain responsive records, including individual officer accounts, group mailboxes, and archived or backup systems. Records should be provided in native electronic format where possible (e.g., .pst, .msg, or .eml with metadata preserved), including all available metadata fields such as sender, recipients, timestamps, and subject lines.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

title: Flock Safety Emails (Bowling Green Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide all emails, attachments, and calendar invites between your agency and Flock Safety (any email communications with the email domain @flocksafety.com) between November 1, 2025 and the date that this request is fulfilled. If Flock sends out mass emails to multiple officers, you may de-duplicate emails to reduce the total number of responsive files.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety Audit - Obstruction
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

On April 4, 2026 at 04:52:52.761Z, a Flock Safety query (eadff215-bbc2-4522-82b7-9c3d05e2d07d) was made on case 26-0000 of 644 unique Flock Safety systems for "Obstructing Justice" 

For this query, please provide:
1) Police Report, Case Records, CAD Notes, Case Notes, or other comments or descriptions related to the case, search, or explanation of how justice was being obstructed and how ALPR data aided in an investigation
2) There are dozens of queries listed as 26-0000, please provide any information related to case 26-0000, or any department policy or guidance that states when this case number should be used for queries (e.g. as a placeholder) 
3) Please provide the user ID and name of the law enforcement officer running the query, along with the role, rank, and department/unit 
4) Please provide the plate number and ALPR detections (hits) returned from the query

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal
